title: Null Move Reductions
text: Home * Search * Selectivity * Reductions * Null Move Reductions Null Move Reductions , not to confused with the widespread Null Move Pruning , work similar to Feldmann's Fail-High Reductions , but use a Null Move Search rather than static evaluation and reduces by a amount of four plies rather than one. Eli David and Nathan S. Netanyahu introduced so called Extended Null-Move Reductions [1] with an adaptive R based on depth . Contents 1 How it works 2 See also 3 Publications 4 Forum Posts 5 References How it works [ edit ] If a null move fail-high occurs - opposed to Null Move Pruning - the search is reduced by four plies, rather than pruned. Thus, Null Move Reductions are therefor less vulnerable to Zugzwang and might even applied in (late) endings. if ( nullMoveAllowed && ...) { R = depth > 6 ? 4 : 3; makeNullMove() score = -zwSearch(1-beta, depth-R-1) // -AlphaBeta (0-beta, 1-beta, depth-R-1) unmakeNullMove(); if (score >= beta ) { depth -= 4; // reduce search if ( depth <= 0 ) return Evaluate(); // Quiescence } } // continue search For zwSearch, see Zero Window Search inside the Principal Variation Search . See also [ edit ] Double Null Move Fail-High Reductions Null Move Observation Null Move Pruning Standing Pat in Quiescence Search Publications [ edit ] Omid David , Nathan S. Netanyahu ( 2008 ). Extended Null-Move Reductions . CG 2008 , pdf Forum Posts [ edit ] Extended Null-Move Reductions by Alvaro Cardoso , CCC , August 20, 2010 Null move alterative in endgames by Aleks Peshkov , CCC , November 16, 2011 thoughts on null-move reduction by Youri Matiounine , CCC , June 14, 2015 » Null Move Pruning References [ edit ] ↑ Omid David , Nathan S. Netanyahu ( 2008 ). Extended Null-Move Reductions . CG 2008 Up one level
